name: boundary_60_risk_on
note: Prompt says 'score >= 60' -- exactly 60 must still qualify.

name: boundary_59_neutral
note: One point below the RISK_ON breadth threshold.

name: boundary_40_neutral
note: Prompt says 'score < 40' for RISK_OFF -- exactly 40 must NOT trigger it.

name: boundary_39_risk_off
note: One point below the RISK_OFF breadth threshold.

name: weak_breadth_alone
note: Weak breadth alone forces RISK_OFF even with clean distribution.

name: strong_breadth_high_distribution
note: Distribution risk must override even excellent breadth -- tests Claude doesn't let a good headline number talk it into more risk.

name: strong_breadth_severe_distribution
note: Same as above with SEVERE instead of HIGH.

name: mid_breadth_caution
note: CAUTION distribution caps at NEUTRAL even though breadth alone would clear the RISK_ON bar.

name: qqq_limitation_clean_spy
qqq_note: QQQ leg returned 403 (known FMP free-tier limitation); SPY-only distribution result used per policy.
note: Tests that the documented QQQ-limitation exception does NOT cause extra degradation when the SPY-only signal is clean.

name: real_world_2026_07_14
qqq_note: QQQ leg returned 403 (known FMP free-tier limitation); SPY-only distribution result used per policy.
note: Mirrors the actual observed 2026-07-14 posture run (SEVERE SPY distribution + neutral breadth -> RISK_OFF).

name: total_skill_failure
note: Both skills produced nothing usable -- prompt's explicit fallback.

name: distribution_skill_totally_failed
qqq_note: ibd-distribution-day-monitor produced no usable data at all (both SPY and QQQ legs failed -- distinct from the known QQQ-only limitation).
note: RISK_ON requires *confirmed* NORMAL distribution, which is impossible to confirm here; the resulting NEUTRAL base then degrades one further notch for total data loss on that skill.
